texto: Animals have come to mean so much in our lives. We live in a fragmented and disconnected culture. Politics are ugly, religion is struggling, technology is stressful, and the economy is unfortunate. What's one thing that we have in our lives that we can depend on? A dog or a cat loving us unconditionally, every day, very faithfully.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Jon Katz que provoca sentido da existência e experiência vivida. Ao questionar “Animals have come to mean so much in our lives. We live in a fragmented and disconnected culture.…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre fe, technology, dog, com foco em termino e dog.

texto: The immature conscience is not its own master. It simply parrots the decisions of others. It does not make judgments of its own; it merely conforms to the judgments of others. That is not real freedom, and it makes true love impossible, for if we are to love truly and freely, we must be able to give something that is truly our own to another. If our heart does not belong to us, asks Merton, how can we give it to another?
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Jon Katz que provoca limites e escolhas conscientes. Ao questionar “The immature conscience is not its own master. It simply parrots the decisions of others. It does…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre amor, conscience, judgement, com foco em judgement e love.

texto: We are human, and we suffer, and unlike the animals on the farm, we are self-aware, and we know that we suffer, and it doesn't hurt more or less if God caused it or could stop it, at least for me. I am definitely of the school that believes God has bigger stuff to worry about than me.

texto: I think if I've learned anything about friendship, it's to hang in, stay connected, fight for them, and let them fight for you. Don't walk away, don't be distracted, don't be too busy or tired, don't take them for granted. Friends are part of the glue that holds life and faith together. Powerful stuff.

texto: It is difficult to see ourselves as we are. Sometimes we are fortunate enough to have good friends, lovers or others who will do us the good service of telling us the truth about ourselves. When we don't, we can so easily delude ourselves, lose a sense of truth about ourselves, and our conscience loses power and purpose. Mostly, we tell ourselves what we would like to hear. We lose our way.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Jon Katz: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “It is difficult to see ourselves as we are. Sometimes we are fortunate enough to have good friend…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, selves, verdade pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de termino e verdade.

texto: When an animal dies, it gives you the chance to love another animal. That's an insightful and profound way to look at it.

texto: Dogs are blameless, devoid of calculation, neither blessed nor cursed with human motives. They can't really be held responsible for what they do. But we can."--from The Dogs of Bedlam Farm

texto: Whenever I hear people clucking about the decline of civilization, what's wrong with young people, how vulgar popular culture is, how confusing and frightening they find the internet, alarms go off. I know I'm around somebody whose hinges are rusting. Death will be bad enough, but for me, this early harbinger is more fearsome, because a part of one's spirit and openness and ability to learn and grow disappears.

texto: It's important to remember that the animals are not grieving with us. They're very accepting. They're not lying there thinking 'How could you do this to me? Why aren't you keeping me going?' Pets don't do the human things of guilt and anger and recrimination that we do. They come and go with great acceptance.

The…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre reflexao, great, thinking, com foco em thinking e acceptance.

texto: Most Americans acquire dogs impulsively and for dubious reasons: as a Christmas gift for the kids. Because they saw one in a movie. To match the new living-room furniture. Because they moved to the suburbs and see a dog as part of the package.
